title: psoralea bituminosa var. plumosa
intro: Psoralea bituminosa var. plumosa is a variant of the Psoralea bituminosa plant, also known as Indian celery, which is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The health benefits of Psoralea bituminosa var. plumosa include its potential to improve skin health, reduce the risk of cancer, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
